description_long: This bungalow is a single-story residential building with a broad, dark gray gable roof and light blue horizontal siding. The front facade features a central door accessed by a small set of steps, flanked by two large windows with white trim and shutters. The side elevations display symmetrical window arrangements, each with white-framed double-hung windows and decorative shutters. The rear of the bungalow includes additional windows and a small attic window beneath the roof peak, maintaining the symmetry and simplicity of the design. The structure sits close to the ground, with no visible porch or veranda, and the overall appearance is neat and well-proportioned. The materials suggest a wood-framed construction with asphalt shingles and painted wood or vinyl siding.
description: A single-story blue bungalow with a dark gable roof, white-trimmed windows, and a central front door accessed by steps. The design is symmetrical and simple, with no porch and decorative shutters on all windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central door, steps, and two large windows with white shutters.
description_view_1: Side gable view with two symmetrically placed windows and a small attic window under the roof peak.
description_view_2: Rear view with three windows, including a larger one on the right, all with white trim and shutters.
description_view_3: Opposite gable end with a central door, steps, and two large windows, mirroring the front but with a different window arrangement.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical front-facing windows with evenly spaced side and rear windows
